title: Residents worried about property tax increase due to $1.8 million plan
summary: Syracuse City officials proposed an $1.8 million, three-year property reassessment project affecting 41,000+ properties with anticipated 70%+ valuation increases. Common Council delayed voting on the project due to governance concerns; residents expressed significant anxiety about resulting property tax increases. The project, managed by Buffalo-based G.A.R. Associates, requires council approval before spring launch.

political_signal.description: Resident opposition to property reassessment plan citing cost-of-living concerns and budget constraints. Council voting delayed due to management/oversight concerns, indicating governance scrutiny.

processing_notes: Dollar amount $1.8 million confirmed in title and content
DPW reference appears only in unrelated 'MORE TO EXPLORE' section; may be false positive
Article focuses on assessment/valuation process rather than CIO-specific tech systems or digital services
No explicit IT infrastructure, GIS system implementation, or digital service delivery components mentioned
